text: Countless icy particles orbiting in a thin disk — not solid hoops. Saturn’s rings are a breathtaking sight, but what are they really made of? The rings stretch over 175,000 miles wide, composed mostly of ice and rock. Some particles are as small as grains of sand, while others can be as big as mountains! These icy bodies create a stunning spectacle, reflecting sunlight in a dazzling display. Curious about more cosmic wonders? Like and follow for more!
